message:
feat(compat): implement real I/O trait bridging and functional hyper executor/timer

Replace placeholder scaffolding in the tokio-compat crate with working
implementations:

hyper_bridge.rs:
- AsupersyncExecutor: replace unimplemented!() with a configurable
  spawn_fn (Arc<dyn Fn(...)>) that routes futures into the correct
  Asupersync region. Add with_spawn_fn() constructor and noop() for
  testing. Implement hyper::rt::Executor<F> for F: Future<Output=()>.
- AsupersyncTimer/AsupersyncSleep: replace busy-polling Instant::now()
  check with proper waker-based sleep using a background timer thread.
  SleepState shared via Arc<Mutex> tracks completion and waker. Thread
  spawned exactly once on first poll, sleeps until deadline, then wakes
  the task.
- Add tests: custom spawn_fn validation, past-deadline immediate Ready,
  future-deadline thread+waker round-trip, timer reset, noop safety.

io.rs:
- Implement all 6 I/O trait bridges (previously just comments):
  - TokioIo<T>: impl tokio::io::AsyncRead/AsyncWrite (feature=tokio-io)
  - AsupersyncIo<T>: impl asupersync::io::AsyncRead/AsyncWrite (feature=tokio-io)
  - TokioIo<T>: impl hyper::rt::Read/Write (feature=hyper-bridge)
- ReadBuf bridging: TokioIo reads into Asupersync ReadBuf then advances
  Tokio ReadBuf; AsupersyncIo does the inverse. hyper direction uses
  unsafe cursor advance matching hyper-util patterns.
- Vectored write and is_write_vectored forwarded in all directions.
- Add comprehensive tests: read/write bridge correctness for both
  directions, flush/shutdown forwarding, round-trip data preservation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
